the .arc file with \+\_\+\+\_\+\+NPX\+\_\+\+\_\+x\+\_\+\+\_\+\+NPY\+\_\+\+\_\+x\+\_\+\+\_\+\+NPZ\+\_\+\+\_\+-\/\+\_\+\+\_\+\+NP\+\_\+\+\_\+p pattern is a generic file where the number of processor in each dimension has to be set. \+\_\+\+\_\+\+NPX\+\_\+\+\_\+, \+\_\+\+\_\+\+NPY\+\_\+\+\_\+, \+\_\+\+\_\+\+NPZ\+\_\+\+\_\+ are the pattern for the number of processors in each dimension x, y and z in the file name and in the file itself\+: 
\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{<nsd>\_\_NPX\_\_ \_\_NPY\_\_ \_\_NPZ\_\_</nsd>}

\end{DoxyCode}
 You can use \mbox{\hyperlink{genPNXY_8py_source}{gen\+PNXY.\+py}} to generates effective .arc file for a defined number of processors {\bfseries{NP}}. {\bfseries{NPZ}} will always be one, {\bfseries{NPX}} and {\bfseries{NPY}} are such as {\bfseries{NPX}} x {\bfseries{NPY}} == {\bfseries{NP}} and as close as possible to square root of {\bfseries{NP}}. An exemple for 720 processes\+: 
\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{python genPNXY.py 720 use-\/case-\/two-\/phase-\/flow-\/IFPSolver-\/2048x2048x10-\/dt10-\/nsd-\/\_\_NPX\_\_x\_\_NPY\_\_x\_\_NPZ\_\_-\/\_\_NP\_\_p.arc"{}}

\end{DoxyCode}
 Will generates a file named\+:

use-\/case-\/two-\/phase-\/flow-\/\+IFPSolver-\/2048x2048x10-\/dt10-\/nsd-\/24x30x1-\/720p.\+arc

with inside\+: 
\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{<nsd>24 30 1</nsd>}

\end{DoxyCode}
\hypertarget{md__tmp_opencode_sharc_test_LargeScaleTwoPhaseFlowSimulation_README_autotoc_md66}{}\doxysection{Test case dimensions}\label{md__tmp_opencode_sharc_test_LargeScaleTwoPhaseFlowSimulation_README_autotoc_md66}
The grid dimension is 2048x2048x10 so the test uses $\sim$41.9e6 cells. You can increase or decrease the number of cells in z dimension as you need. To change number of cells in z dimension change 
\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{<origine>0 0 -\/100</origine>}

\end{DoxyCode}
 to 
\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{<origine>0 0 -\/1000</origine}

\end{DoxyCode}
 and 
\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{<lz nz="{}10"{}>100</lz>}

\end{DoxyCode}
 to 
\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{<lz nz="{}100"{}>1000</lz>}

\end{DoxyCode}
 the second number is the dimension in meters.

The case has been tested up to 2048x2048x170 which correspond to 713e6 cells. This grid size generates a linear system which number of equations is closed to the maximum supported by Alien and used Linear solver libraries.

For benchmarking purpose you can reduce the physical simulation time. \mbox{\hyperlink{structIn}{In}} the .arc file the \char`\"{}end\char`\"{} time is given in seconds by 
\begin{DoxyCode}{0}
\DoxyCodeLine{<end-\/time>1728000e3</end-\/time>}

\end{DoxyCode}
 which correspond to 20000 days.
